subtopic: LLM-powered autonomous agents — components and techniques
framing: In an LLM-powered autonomous agent, the LLM is the brain, complemented by three components: Planning, Memory, and Tool use. This post catalogs the techniques used inside each component.

subgoal_and_decomposition.summary: Prompt the model to think step-by-step, decomposing hard tasks into simpler intermediate steps.
Extends CoT by exploring multiple reasoning branches per step (BFS/DFS), evaluated by classifier or majority vote.
LLM translates the problem into PDDL, hands it to an external classical planner, then translates the plan back to natural language.

self_reflection.summary: Interleaves explicit Thought / Action / Observation steps so the LLM reasons in language while acting via discrete tool actions.
Adds dynamic memory and self-reflection: after each trajectory the agent generates a verbal reflection that is injected into working memory for future attempts.
Fine-tunes a model on sequences of past outputs annotated with feedback so it learns to self-improve in subsequent generations.
Distills the learning history of an RL algorithm into a long history-conditioned policy via behavioral cloning.

types.name: Sensory memory
types.llm_analog: Embedding representations of raw inputs (text, image, etc.)

types.name: Short-term / working memory
types.llm_analog: In-context learning — bounded by Transformer context window

types.name: Long-term memory
types.llm_analog: External vector store accessed via fast retrieval (MIPS)

fast_retrieval_algorithms_MIPS.name: LSH (Locality-Sensitive Hashing)
fast_retrieval_algorithms_MIPS.summary: Hash function maps similar items to the same bucket with high probability.

fast_retrieval_algorithms_MIPS.name: ANNOY
fast_retrieval_algorithms_MIPS.summary: Random projection trees; each non-leaf splits the space with a hyperplane.

fast_retrieval_algorithms_MIPS.name: HNSW (Hierarchical Navigable Small World)
fast_retrieval_algorithms_MIPS.summary: Hierarchical small-world graphs; navigates from coarse top layers to fine bottom layers.

fast_retrieval_algorithms_MIPS.name: FAISS
fast_retrieval_algorithms_MIPS.summary: Vector quantization with cluster partitioning, then refined quantization within clusters.

fast_retrieval_algorithms_MIPS.name: ScaNN
fast_retrieval_algorithms_MIPS.summary: Anisotropic vector quantization optimized for inner-product similarity preservation.

tool_use.summary: Neuro-symbolic architecture: a general LLM routes queries to expert modules (neural or symbolic: calculator, currency, weather, etc.).
Fine-tunes an LM to learn to use external tool APIs.
Self-supervised: the model learns which APIs to call by checking whether the API call improves output likelihood.
Practical LLM-tool augmentation with developer- or self-defined tool APIs.
Four stages — task planning, model selection, task execution, response generation — using ChatGPT to orchestrate HuggingFace models.
Benchmark with 53 APIs and 264 dialogues; evaluates whether to call, which API, and multi-step API planning.

case_studies.name: ChemCrow
case_studies.summary: Domain LLM agent augmented with 13 chemistry tools for organic synthesis, drug discovery, materials design.

case_studies.name: Boiko et al. 2023
case_studies.summary: LLM agents that browse the web, read docs, run code, call lab robotics APIs for autonomous scientific experiments.

case_studies.name: Generative Agents
case_studies.summary: 25 LLM-powered virtual characters with memory stream, retrieval (recency/importance/relevance), reflection, and planning — produce emergent social behavior.

case_studies.name: AutoGPT
case_studies.summary: Proof-of-concept autonomous agent with goal-driven loop; mostly format-parsing code.

case_studies.name: GPT-Engineer
case_studies.summary: Generates entire code repositories from a natural-language task; asks for clarification, then writes files.

challenges_called_out_by_source: Finite context length limits historical info, instructions, and tool context.
Long-term planning and task decomposition: LLMs struggle to adjust plans when faced with unexpected errors.
Reliability of natural-language interfaces: LLMs make formatting errors and may refuse instructions, forcing heavy output-parsing code.
note_on_recency: This post is from June 2023. Many of its techniques (ReAct, CoT, ToT, function calling) are now standard. The component decomposition (Planning / Memory / Tool use) remains the most-cited mental model for LLM agents.
